subject: Clerical Office Duties
preamble: This syllabus covers the principles, procedures, equipment, and personal habits essential for performing clerical duties effectively in a modern business office environment.
objectives: Understand the principles and procedures in performing clerical duties in an office.
Apply knowledge acquired on the uses of office equipment and materials to perform basic clerical duties.
Communicate effectively within an organizational context.
Explain basic personal and professional habits and attitudes necessary for the performance of office duties.
Develop problem-solving skills to deal with clerical and secretarial issues in the office.
Provide a solid foundation for further courses in business studies.

examination_scheme.summary: There will be two papers, Paper 1 and Paper 2, both of which must be taken.

papers.title: Objective Questions
papers.details: Consists of fifty compulsory multiple-choice objective questions covering the entire syllabus.

papers.title: Case Study & Essay Questions
papers.details: Consists of one compulsory case study based on office situations (22 marks) and six essay questions, out of which candidates must answer any three (16 marks each).

topics.title: THE OFFICE
details: Definition and functions of an office (Receiving, Recording, Processing/Analysing, Giving, Storing information, Safeguarding Assets).
Sections of an office (Reception, General Office, Stores, Mail Room/Registry) and their roles.
Departments (Accounts, Purchasing, Sales, HR, Production, Legal, Administration, Transport) and departmental interrelationships.
Office Personnel roles (Office Manager, Supervisor, Clerks, Secretaries, Receptionist, Typist, Messenger) and secretary classifications.
Professional Image (Personal and business attributes, social habits, personal hygiene, and time management skills/wasters).
Office Layout (Open and closed layouts, factors determining choice, planning reasons, advantages, and disadvantages).

topics.title: ORGANISATION
details: Principles of Organisation (Unity of objectives, Span of control, Authority/Responsibility, Delegation, Unity of command, Supervision, Remuneration, Esprit de corps).
Organisational Structures (Line, Line and staff, Staff, Functional, Matrix).
Organisational Chart/Organogram: Construction, uses, advantages, and disadvantages.

topics.title: BUSINESS TRANSACTIONS
details: Documents used in transactions (Enquiry, Quotation, LPO, Proforma Invoice, Delivery Note/Waybill, Debit/Credit Note, Statement of Account, Receipts, etc.).
Methods of Payment (Cash, Cheque, Standing order, Credit transfer, Money transfer) with advantages and disadvantages.
Payroll Handling: Terminologies (Gross pay, Net pay, PAYE), methods of determining workers' pay (piece rate, time rate, commission, overtime), and payroll preparation.
Tax Regimes: Types of taxes (VAT, Income Tax, CST, Property Tax, Customs Duty) and their importance.
Petty Cash and the Imprest system: Preparation and maintenance of the petty cash book.

topics.title: BANKING
details: Types of Banks: Functions of Commercial, Development, Merchant, Rural/Community Banks, and the Central Bank.
Types of Accounts: Features of Savings, Current, and Fixed Deposit accounts.
Cheques: Definition, types, valid cheque features, parties involved, reasons for dishonouring, and cheque crossings.

topics.title: STOCK PROCEDURE
details: Ordering Stock: Meaning of stock, importance of stockkeeping, and ordering procedures.
Storekeeper Duties and requirements for a efficient stock system.
Requisition Procedure and stock record documentation (Bin cards, tally cards, purchase orders).
Stocktaking methods (Annual, Periodic, Perpetual) and Stock Control levels (Minimum, Maximum, Re-order level).
Stock Valuation methods (Cost price, Average price, Market price, Lower price).

topics.title: OFFICE EQUIPMENT AND MACHINES
details: General Office Machines (Computers, Photocopiers, Dictaphones, Duplicators, Calculators, Cash Registers, Scanners): Selection factors, manual vs. electric machines, and maintenance.
Computers and ICT: Components, types, peripheral devices, internet/intranet, WWW, and service providers.
Mail Room Equipment (Franking machines, Shredders, Letter openers, Scales, Guillotines, Staplers).

topics.title: COMMUNICATION
details: Forms and Channels of Communication (Verbal, Non-verbal, Visual; Vertical, Horizontal, Diagonal).
Barriers to Effective Communication (Organisational, Physical, Psychological, Socio-Cultural).
Business Correspondence: Letters, Circulars, Memos, and Report types (Short formal/informal, Routine, Special, Statutory).
Mail Handling: Incoming and outgoing mail procedures, registers, postage books, and messenger receipts.
Meetings: Kinds, requirements for valid meetings, officials' duties, terminologies, and drafting documents (Notice, Agenda, Minutes).

topics.title: BUSINESS ENGLISH
details: Proofreading and Office Abbreviations: Purpose of proofreading, correcting passage errors, expanding abbreviations.
Rules for spelling, word division, and punctuation.

topics.title: FILING AND INDEXING
details: Classification Methods (Alphabetical, Numerical, Subject, Geographical, Chronological, Alpha-numeric).
Filing Systems (Lateral, Suspension, Vertical, Horizontal) and rules for good filing.
Indexing and Cross-Referencing: Types, forms, and importance.
Electronic Filing (including microfilming/micrographics) and Centralized vs. Departmental filing.

topics.title: REFERENCE BOOKS AND SOURCES OF INFORMATION
details: Reference Books: Dictionaries, Encyclopaedias, Atlases, Trade Journals, Telephone Directories, Government Publications (Gazette, Hansard, White paper).
Sources of Information: Personal/Driving documents, Passport, Visa, Tax Clearance.
Business Information sources: Exchange rates, stock exchange listings, bank financial bulletins.

topics.title: POST OFFICE SERVICES
details: Postal Services: Express mail, Special mail, Poste restante, Private mail bag/box, Registered mail, Business reply service.
Courier Services (EMS, DHL, FedEx) and Post Office Agency/Remittance services (Postal Orders, Money Transfers).

topics.title: HUMAN RELATIONS
details: Internal Office Relations and Public Relations: Factors promoting goodwill and strategies for improving interpersonal skills.

topics.title: HEALTH, SAFETY AND WELFARE IN THE OFFICE
details: Health hazards and office accidents: Prevention measures.
Staff Welfare services, benefits, and their advantages.

topics.title: PRE-EMPLOYMENT PROCEDURES
details: Designing job advertisements.
Writing letters of application and preparing a Curriculum Vitae (CV).
Interview preparation techniques.

topics.title: ENTREPRENEURSHIP AND SMALL SCALE BUSINESS
details: Characteristics of an entrepreneur.
Features and importance of small-scale enterprises in society.

reading_list: John Harrison - Secretarial Duties (Pitman Publishing Ltd.)
Elendu O. Elendu - Office Practice for Colleges (New Africa Publishing Co. Ltd. Owerri, 1985)
Sheila May - Case Studies, A Skills-Based Approach (Pitman Publishing Ltd.)
John Harrison - Office Procedure (Pitman Publishing Ltd.)
